page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor operating at 2.2 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A key hardware feature of the m5ad.large is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 75 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage provides direct-attached, low-latency disk space. Networking capabilities include a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the m5ad.large falls into the bottom 10% of performance across most categories. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores are both 590.4. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 720.0 and a multi-core score of 955.0. Memory bandwidth is limited, with large block read-only speeds at 20.35 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.91 GB/sec. Passmark database operations score at 499.81, and Redis SET operations reach 1.26 million operations per second.

Given its hardware profile, the m5ad.large offers a balanced resource density of vCPU to memory, but its compute and memory bandwidth performance are constrained compared to newer generation hardware. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads requiring temporary high-speed scratch space. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, small database operations, light text processing, and general-purpose development tasks that do not require high CPU or memory throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results place the instance in the bottom 10% for CPU and memory performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 720.0 and multi-core score of 955.0. The local NVMe storage provides low-latency temporary space, making the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring local scratch disk. It is suited for basic web serving, light database operations, and general-purpose development tasks that do not demand high compute or memory throughput.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and integrated low-latency NVMe SSD local storage.

Bundled 75 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
